pullRequest.title: Support garbage collection
pullRequest.body: Fixes #92

pullRequest.title: Fix Unicode filename encoding on Windows
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #203 (hopefully) where filenames containing Unicode characters like 'À' were incorrectly encoded/decoded on Windows, resulting in corruption during clone and checkout operations.

The fix adds proper handling of UTF-8 encoded tree paths on Windows by:
- Converting UTF-8 tree paths to filesystem encoding when creating files
- Converting filesystem paths back to UTF-8 when storing in git trees
- Adding a tree_encoding parameter (defaulting to 'utf-8') to relevant functions for future flexibility

Added comprehensive test coverage for Unicode filename handling.

pullRequest.title: Add support for core.protectHFS configuration setting
pullRequest.body:
Implement Git's core.protectHFS setting to protect against paths that could be misinterpreted on HFS+ filesystems through Unicode normalization. This includes filtering HFS+ ignorable characters and applying NFD normalization to detect malicious path components.

Fixes #246

pullRequest.title: ParamikoSSHVendor: Add support for SSH config file
pullRequest.body:
The ParamikoSSHVendor now reads SSH configuration from ~/.ssh/config and uses host-specific settings when establishing connections. This includes hostname aliases, user, port, and identity file settings.

Fixes #443

pullRequest.title: Update working tree in pull
pullRequest.body: Fixes #452

pullRequest.title: Add porcelain submodule commands
pullRequest.body:
- Implement submodule_update() in porcelain to clone/fetch and checkout submodules
- Add CLI commands: submodule add, submodule update
- Add --recurse-submodules option to clone command

Fixes #506

pullRequest.title: Add test for porcelain.add() from ignored directory
pullRequest.body:
Add regression test to verify that porcelain.add() correctly handles adding files when the current working directory is within a gitignored directory. 

Fixes #550

pullRequest.title: Add prune command
pullRequest.body: Fixes #558

pullRequest.title: Implement dulwich.porcelain.checkout
pullRequest.body: Fixes #576

pullRequest.title: Add test for porcelain.push return value
pullRequest.body:
Verify that porcelain.push returns SendPackResult with per-ref status information. This functionality was already implemented but lacked test coverage.

Fixes #780

pullRequest.title: Fix porcelain.add() to handle symlinks pointing outside repository
pullRequest.body:
Previously, porcelain.add() would fail when trying to add a symlink that points outside the repository because path.resolve() would fully resolve the symlink, causing a ValueError when the resolved path was outside the repo boundaries.

This fix changes the path resolution logic to only resolve the parent directory for symlinks, keeping the symlink itself unresolved. This matches Git's behavior which allows adding symlinks regardless of where they point.

Fixes #789

pullRequest.title: Fix KeyError when pulling from a shallow clone
pullRequest.body:
Handle missing commits gracefully in graph traversal operations for shallow repositories. When operating on shallow clones, commits referenced as parents may not exist in the repository. This change:

Fixes #813

pullRequest.title: Fix porcelain.remove() to work from any directory
pullRequest.body:
Previously, porcelain.remove() would fail when called from a directory other than the repository root because it interpreted relative paths as relative to the current working directory. This change makes relative paths relative to the repository root.

Fixes #821

pullRequest.title: Add callback-based authentication support
pullRequest.body:
Implement callback-based authentication for HTTP and proxy authentication in Urllib3HttpGitClient. This provides a cleaner alternative to exception interception for handling authentication challenges.

The implementation wraps the urllib3 pool manager with AuthCallbackPoolManager which intercepts 401/407 responses and invokes the appropriate callbacks.

Fixes #822

pullRequest.title: Fix porcelain.describe() hash length to match git describe behavior
pullRequest.body:
- Replace hardcoded 7-character hash with dynamic length based on uniqueness
- Update find_unique_abbrev to find shortest unique prefix (minimum 7 chars)

Fixes #824

pullRequest.title: porcelain.status: Support untracked_files="normal" and make it the default
pullRequest.body: Fixes #835

pullRequest.title: Fix porcelain.reset --hard to delete files not in target tree
pullRequest.body:
When resetting to a remote branch, files that were deleted in the remote were not being removed locally due to incorrect path normalization on Windows. The issue was that paths were being compared with different separators (backslash vs forward slash).

This fix normalizes paths to use forward slashes consistently, matching Git's internal representation, ensuring files are properly deleted when they don't exist in the target tree.

Fixes #840

pullRequest.title: Return symrefs from ls_refs
pullRequest.body: Fixes #863

pullRequest.title: Add per-URL http.extraHeader configuration support
pullRequest.body: Fixes #882

pullRequest.title: Two fixes for add handling
pullRequest.body:
* Fix ``porcelain.add()`` when adding repository root path or directories.
  Previously, adding the repository path itself would incorrectly stage
  ``b'./'`` instead of the actual untracked files, leading to
  repository corruption.  Fixed #1178

* Improve ``porcelain.add()`` documentation to correctly describe default
  behavior. Fixes #895

pullRequest.title: docs: Clarify trailing slash requirement for directory ignore checks
pullRequest.body:
When checking if directories are ignored, users must include a trailing slash (e.g., "dir/" instead of "dir") to get correct results. This matches Git's behavior.

Added compatibility test to verify behavior matches Git.

Fixes #972

pullRequest.title: Fix TypeError when handling bytes paths in FileSystemBackend

Functions that accept file paths now support pathlib.Path objects in addition to strings and bytes. This includes repository operations, configuration file handling, ignore file processing, and all major entry points.

Fixes #1074

pullRequest.body: Fixes #1088

pullRequest.title: Fix handling of fetching of packs over dumb http transports

pullRequest.title: Add comprehensive SHA256 support for Git repositories
pullRequest.body:
- Add hash algorithm abstraction layer (dulwich/hash.py)
- Support SHA256 object IDs in refs, object store, and repository
- Fix tree parsing to handle both SHA1 (20-byte) and SHA256 (32-byte) hashes
- Update DiskObjectStore to use correct hash algorithm for object storage
- Add comprehensive compatibility tests for SHA256 repositories
- Enable full interoperability between Dulwich and Git for SHA256 repos

Fixes #1115

pullRequest.title: Add progress reporting during pack file downloads
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1121

pullRequest.title: Fix support for short commit hashes in porcelain.reset()
pullRequest.body:
Modified parse_tree() to handle short commit hashes by falling back to parse_commit() when direct repository lookup fails. This resolves issue

Fixes #1154

pullRequest.title: Fix LocalGitClient assertion error with MemoryRepo
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1179

pullRequest.title: Fix config file leakage in compat tests
pullRequest.body:
On systems with Apple Git (macOS with Xcode Command Line Tools), the system-wide git config sets init.defaultBranch=main in: /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/share/git-core/gitconfig

This causes compat tests to fail with "KeyError: b'HEAD'" because:
1. git init --bare creates HEAD pointing to refs/heads/main
2. git fast-import with test exports creates refs/heads/master
3. HEAD points to non-existent refs/heads/main
4. Dulwich fails when trying to resolve HEAD

The fix isolates tests from system and user git configuration by setting:
- GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 to ignore system-wide config
- GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null to ignore user's ~/.gitconfig

This requires git 2.32.0+ (released June 2021) for GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL support, so the minimum git version for compat tests is updated from 1.5.0 to 2.32.0.

Fixes #1188

pullRequest.title: Add support for GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL and GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM environment variables
pullRequest.body:
These variables allow overriding the default global and system configuration paths.

Fixes #1193

pullRequest.title: Fix gitignore pattern matching for directory negation patterns
pullRequest.body:
Patterns like ``!data/*/`` now correctly unignore direct subdirectories while still ignoring files in the parent directory, matching Git's behavior. The ``is_ignored()`` method now documents that directory paths should end with ``/`` for consistent behavior.

Also, support quote_path flag.

Fixes #1203

pullRequest.title: Add comprehensive include/includeIf support to config parser
pullRequest.body:
This implements full support for Git's include and includeIf directives:

Features:
- Basic include functionality with path resolution
- includeIf with gitdir condition evaluation (including pattern matching)
- Circular include detection and prevention
- Include depth limiting to prevent infinite recursion
- Proper handling of relative and absolute include paths
- Case-insensitive gitdir matching (gitdir/i)
- Graceful handling of missing include files

The implementation follows Git's behavior as documented in git-config(1) and supports the most commonly used include scenarios.

Fixes #1216

pullRequest.title: Improve bundle handling
pullRequest.body:
* Add ``BundleClient`` for interacting with bundles like a Git client
* Add ``create_bundle_from_repo`` for easily creating bundles

Fixes #1246

pullRequest.title: Add test for issue #1247 (top-level untracked files detection)
pullRequest.body:
The bug described in issue #1247 where top-level untracked files were not detected by get_untracked_paths() and status() appears to have already been fixed. Adding a specific test case to prevent regression.

Fixes #1247

pullRequest.title: Restore macOS x86_64 wheel builds
pullRequest.body:
Remove obsolete ARCHFLAGS ppc workaround from setup.py that could interfere with architecture-specific compilation

Fixes #1303

pullRequest.title: Fix Rust implementation of sorted_tree_items() for submodules (#1325)
pullRequest.body:
The Rust implementation was incorrectly treating submodules (mode 0o160000) as directories during tree sorting. This happened because it used a simple bitwise AND check (mode & S_IFDIR != 0) which matches both directories (0o40000) and submodules (0o160000).

Fixed by properly checking the file type using the S_IFMT mask, matching the Python implementation's behavior with stat.S_ISDIR().

Added test case that reproduces the issue and verifies both implementations now produce identical sorting results.

Fixes #1325

pullRequest.body: Fixes #1366

Add revert functionality to dulwich.porcelain and CLI that creates new commits to undo changes from specified commits while preserving history.

Previously, checking out an annotated tag would fail with a KeyError because parse_commit() didn't dereference tag objects to their target commits. This fix adds tag dereferencing to all code paths in parse_commit(), ensuring that annotated tags are properly resolved to their target commits.

Previously, dulwich would write empty extensions to the index file, causing unnecessary bloat. Now we filter out extensions with no data before writing.

pullRequest.body:

Reduce complexity while maintaining exact Git-compatible behavior:
- Streamline _check_parent_exclusion function by combining conditions

pullRequest.body:
Previously, Dulwich would load multiple gitconfig files on Windows including defunct ones from old installations.

Changes:
- Modified get_win_system_paths() to only return the current Git config path
- Added get_win_legacy_system_paths() for diagnostic/migration purposes
- Added debug logging to show which gitconfig files are loaded

Fixes #1732

pullRequest.body:
The worktree CLI commands expect to be run from within a repository directory. Updated the test methods to change to the repo directory before running commands and restore the original directory afterward.

Fixes #1738

pullRequest.title: Add `object_store.iter_commit_contents()`
pullRequest.body:
Closes #1740.

I also added a new section to the documentation, "recipes", and added recipes demonstrating the use of `iter_commit_contents()` and `tree_lookup_path()` to retrieve files from the repository.

pullRequest.title: Add colorized diff support for show command
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1741

pullRequest.title: Fix Windows path handling regression in checkout operations
pullRequest.body:
Fix a regression introduced in 0.23.1 where checkout operations on Windows would fail with "Cannot create directory, parent path is a file: b'C:'" error.

The issue occurred in _ensure_parent_dir_exists when validating parent paths. The original implementation manually split paths and reconstructed them, which didn't correctly handle Windows drive letters or UNC paths.

The fix uses os.path.dirname() to properly walk up the directory tree, letting Python's built-in path handling deal with all platform-specific cases including:
- Windows drive letters (C:\, D:\, etc.)
- UNC paths (\\server\share\...)
- Unix paths (/home/user/...)

This approach is more robust and eliminates the need for platform-specific special cases.

Fixes #1751

pullRequest.title: Fix merge functionality to handle missing merge3 dependency gracefully
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1759

pullRequest.title: Add support for http.extraHeader configuration
pullRequest.body:
This allows passing additional HTTP headers to the server when communicating over HTTP(S), following Git's standard configuration pattern. Users can set single or multiple headers via:

  git config --add http.extraHeader "Authorization: Bearer token"
  git config --add http.extraHeader "X-Custom-Header: value"

The headers are parsed from the config and applied to all HTTP requests made by the client.

Fixes #1769

pullRequest.title: Fix handling of CRLF line endings with core.autocrlf = input
pullRequest.body:
When core.autocrlf = input is set, files with CRLF line endings were incorrectly showing as unstaged even when their content hadn't changed. This was because TreeBlobNormalizer.checkin_normalize() was not applying the clean filter (CRLF to LF conversion) for existing files when autocrlf=input was set.

The fix ensures that when autocrlf=input is configured, all text files are normalized during checkin operations, including when checking status. This makes Dulwich's behavior consistent with Git.

Fixes #1770

pullRequest.title: Add dulwich config CLI command
pullRequest.body:
Supported operations:
- Get configuration values
- Set configuration values
- List all configuration
- Get all values for multivars
- Unset configuration values

Fixes #1775

pullRequest.title: Add support for atomic push operations (--atomic)
pullRequest.body:
When enabled, either all ref updates in a push succeed or none are applied. Both client and server now negotiate the "atomic" capability per the Git protocol specification.

Closes #1781

pullRequest.title: Add support for git cherry command
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1782

pullRequest.title: Implement advanced Git object specification support
pullRequest.body:
Add support for two advanced Git object specification syntaxes:

1. Index path lookup (:path) - Access files from the Git index at specific merge conflict stages:
   - :path or :0:path - stage 0 (current index)
   - :1:path - stage 1 (common ancestor)
   - :2:path - stage 2 (current branch/this)
   - :3:path - stage 3 (other branch)

2. Reflog time specifications (@{time}) - Query repository state at specific points in time using Git's approxidate format:
   - HEAD@{yesterday}
   - master@{2.weeks.ago}
   - @{1234567890} (Unix timestamp)
   - @{2009-02-13} (ISO date)

Fixes #1783

pullRequest.title: Add support for rename, copy, and binary patches in git apply
pullRequest.body:
- Rename detection: Parses and applies 'rename from/to' headers
- Copy detection: Parses and applies 'copy from/to' headers
- Binary patches: Implements Git's base85 decoder for binary patch data
- 3-way merge: Adds --3way/-3 option to fall back to three-way merge when patches don't apply cleanly

Fixes #1784

pullRequest.title: Add rerere support
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1786

pullRequest.title: Add basic midx support
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1787

pullRequest.title: Add maintenance subcommand
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1788

pullRequest.title: Optimize LFS performance by avoiding redundant disk writes
pullRequest.body:
When checking file status, the LFS filter's clean method was writing every file to the LFS object store, even for unchanged files. This caused severe performance degradation for large repositories.

The fix optimizes LFSStore.write_object to:
- First compute the SHA256 hash
- Check if the object already exists
- Only write to disk if the object doesn't exist

This avoids redundant disk I/O for unchanged files during status checks, significantly improving performance in repositories with many LFS-tracked files.

Fixes #1789

pullRequest.title: Fix subprocess filter performance issues (#1789)
pullRequest.body:
The ProcessFilterDriver was spawning a new subprocess for every file operation, causing severe performance degradation for large repositories (minutes instead of milliseconds for git status).

This commit implements the Git filter process protocol to use a single long-running process for all filter operations:

- Add support for filter.<driver>.process configuration
- Implement Git filter process protocol (version 2) with proper pkt-line format

Fixes #1789

pullRequest.title: Use bitmap support
pullRequest.body:
Actually use bitmap support and support creating bitmap files.

Fixes #1792

pullRequest.title: Add Git protocol v2 packfile-uris client support
pullRequest.body:
Implement client-side support for the packfile-uris capability in Git protocol v2, which allows servers to offload packfile distribution to CDNs or external storage (e.g., Google Cloud Storage, AWS S3).

Fixes #1794

pullRequest.body: Fixes #1795

pullRequest.title: Add IPv6 support for git:// protocol URLs
pullRequest.body:
The TCPGitClient class now properly formats IPv6 addresses in git:// URLs by wrapping them in square brackets as required by RFC 3986. The underlying socket connection already supported IPv6 through AF_UNSPEC.

Fixes #1796

pullRequest.title: Add sparse index support for improved performance with large repositories
pullRequest.body:
- Read and write sparse directory entries with SKIP_WORKTREE flag
- Index expansion (sparse -> full) and collapse (full -> sparse) operations

pullRequest.title: Add support for core.precomposeunicode configuration option
pullRequest.body:
On macOS, HFS+/APFS filesystems return filenames in NFD (decomposed) Unicode form. When core.precomposeunicode is true, normalize filesystem paths to NFC before comparing with index entries, preventing tracked files from appearing as untracked.

Fixes #1804

pullRequest.title: Add support for core.whitespace and core.safecrlf
pullRequest.body:

* Add core.safecrlf configuration support for safe CRLF conversion checks

Fixes #1806

pullRequest.title: Extend ignorecase and precomposeunicode support to index lookups
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1807

pullRequest.title: Add support for GIT_FLUSH environment variable
pullRequest.body:
When GIT_FLUSH=1, output is flushed after each write operation, enabling real-time output visibility for CI/CD systems and scripts that parse Git output as it's generated.

When GIT_FLUSH=0, output uses completely buffered I/O.

When GIT_FLUSH is not set, the behavior is auto-detected based on whether stdout/stderr are connected to a TTY (no flush) or redirected to a pipe/file (flush enabled).

Fixes #1810

pullRequest.title: Add support for core.preloadIndex configuration setting
pullRequest.body:
This setting enables parallel stat operations when checking for unstaged changes, improving performance on slow filesystems like NFS.

When core.preloadIndex is enabled, get_unstaged_changes() uses a thread pool to parallelize filesystem operations. The implementation:
- Uses ThreadPoolExecutor with up to 8 workers (or CPU count if smaller)
- Falls back to serial processing if threading is unavailable
- Maintains compatibility by defaulting to serial processing

The setting is respected in both porcelain.status() and porcelain.add() operations.

Fixes https://github.com/jelmer/dulwich/issues/1851

pullRequest.title: Add support for gc.pruneExpire configuration option
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1859

pullRequest.title: Add support for GIT_TRACE environment variable
pullRequest.body:
Supported values:
- "1", "2", "true": Output to stderr with DEBUG level
- "3" through "9": Output to file descriptor
- Absolute path: Append to file
- Directory: Create per-process trace files (trace.{pid})
- "0", "false", empty, unset: Disabled

Fixes #1863

pullRequest.title: Support TMPDIR as example system directory in symlink test
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1885

pullRequest.title: Fix Git filter protocol to handle two-phase response format
pullRequest.body:
The Git long-running process filter protocol specifies a two-phase response format where filters send:
1. Initial headers with status
2. Content data
3. Final headers with status (potentially different from initial)

The previous implementation only read the initial headers and content, causing protocol sync issues when working with filters like Git LFS that properly implement the two-phase protocol.

Fixes #1889

pullRequest.title: Use immutable container type annotations where appropriate
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1894

pullRequest.title: Fix HTTP authentication with credentials in URLs
pullRequest.body:
When credentials are embedded in a URL (like https://token@github.com), preserve them when storing the remote URL in git config. This ensures that subsequent operations like push can authenticate successfully.

Fixes #1925

pullRequest.title: Restore pool_manager parameter to transport functions
pullRequest.body:
The pool_manager parameter was accidentally removed from get_transport_and_path and get_transport_and_path_from_url during type annotation refactoring in commit 0b0fed6. This restores the parameter to maintain backward compatibility.

Fixes #1928

pullRequest.title: Fix import failure when sys.stdin is None
pullRequest.body: Fixes #1939

pullRequest.body:
Regression from 0b0fed6e

pullRequest.title: Work around typing module bug in Python 3.9.0/3.9.1
pullRequest.body:
Use string annotation for Callable type in reflog.py to avoid triggering a bug in Python 3.9.0 and 3.9.1's typing module that fails when processing Callable[[bytes], bool] annotations.

Fixes #1948

pullRequest.title: Bump typing-extensions
pullRequest.body:
This is necessary because we now use Buffer

Fixes #1973

pullRequest.title: Optimize status performance by using stat matching to skip unchanged files
pullRequest.body:
This should help with #1999 where dulwich status with LFS filters was very slow.

This matches Git's behavior - Git uses stat matching to avoid expensive filter operations on unchanged files. When autocrlf config is changed after files are committed, Git also doesn't show them as modified until the files are actually touched or explicitly renormalized.

Fixes #1999

pullRequest.title: Move peeled tags protocol functions to dulwich.protocol
pullRequest.body:
Remove InfoRefsContainer class, inlining its functionality into SwiftInfoRefsContainer in dulwich.contrib.swift.

This properly isolates the ^{} protocol syntax to protocol-level code, which is a bit of an architectural leak.

Fixes #2009

pullRequest.title: Fix get_unstaged_changes filter callback to expect Blob objects
pullRequest.body: Fixes #2010

pullRequest.body: Fixes #2023

pullRequest.title: Fix ParamikoSSHVendor interface compatibility with SSHVendor
pullRequest.body: Fixes #2028

pullRequest.title: Fix commit graph parsing with incomplete extra edges data
pullRequest.body:
Fixes parsing failure when EXTRA_EDGE_LIST chunk has less than 4 bytes remaining. This occurred with commits having 3+ parents (octopus merges) when the chunk data was incomplete or malformed.

Fixes #2054

pullRequest.title: Fix cloning of SHA-256 repositories with protocol v2
pullRequest.body:
Fixes cloning from SHA-256 repositories like:
  dulwich clone https://codeberg.org/jfch/test-sha256.git

Fixes #2072

pullRequest.title: Fix client sending unsupported unborn argument to older git servers
pullRequest.body: Fixes #2104

pullRequest.title: Add support for extensions.relativeworktrees
pullRequest.body:
Add relativeworktrees to the list of supported repository extensions. This extension allows Git worktrees to use relative paths instead of absolute paths when linking worktrees.

Dulwich already handles relative worktree paths correctly in its worktree implementation, so this change only adds the extension to the allowlist to prevent UnsupportedExtension errors.

Fixes #2112

pullRequest.title: Fix gitignore parent re-include handling
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #2141.

## Summary
- update parent-exclusion handling so a later `!dir/` re-include can allow a subsequent file-level negation to take effect
- ignore duplicate matches of the same pattern object that can be produced during path traversal
- add a regression test for `dist/`, `!dist/`, `dist/*`, `!dist/keep.txt`

## Tests
- `python3 -m unittest tests.test_ignore`
- `python3 -m ruff check dulwich/ignore.py tests/test_ignore.py`
- `python3 -m ruff format --check dulwich/ignore.py tests/test_ignore.py`

pullRequest.title: midx: Deduplicate objects across packs when writing
pullRequest.body:
Objects can be present in multiple packs (e.g. after git gc creates a cruft pack). The previous writer emitted the same SHA multiple times in the OIDL chunk, which Git rejects with "oid lookup out of order" since that chunk requires strictly increasing OIDs.

Keep the occurrence from the first pack in pack-name order, matching how Git itself resolves duplicates.

Fixes #2152
